description:
libc::pipe2 is Linux-only. balls 0.3.2 builds fine on Linux but fails to compile on macOS because src/plugin/diag.rs:prepare_diag_pipe calls pipe2(O_CLOEXEC). Reported while trying to use balls on macOS.

Fix: split make_pipe by cfg(target_os). On Linux, keep pipe2 (atomic CLOEXEC). On macOS/BSD, fall back to pipe() + fcntl(FD_CLOEXEC). Balls only calls prepare_diag_pipe from the synchronous plugin-invocation path, so the classical pipe+fcntl race against another thread's fork/exec isn't reachable.

Keep 100% coverage on Linux. The Linux-only pipe2 error-branch test can stay cfg-gated; the macOS branch isn't measured by tarpaulin in CI anyway but must compile cleanly.
title: Cross-platform diag pipe: fall back to pipe+fcntl off Linux
